creator: Julie Bort
title: (No AI) OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
dc:creator: Julie Bort
content: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.
contentSnippet: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

creator: Anna Heim
title: (No AI) DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
dc:creator: Anna Heim
content: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.
contentSnippet: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

title: (No AI) Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A vintage computer on a background of 1s and 0s with a brain on the screen representing AI](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/09/STK_414_AI_CHATBOT_R2_CVirginia_D.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

One of the most popular Linux distributions is about to get an influx of AI features. As reported by [_Phoronix_](https://www.phoronix.com/news/Ubuntu-AI-Features-2026), Jon Seager, VP of engineering at Ubuntu developer Canonical, [shared a blog post](https://discourse.ubuntu.com/t/the-future-of-ai-in-ubuntu/81130) on Monday detailing plans to add AI features to the Linux distro over the next year. As the post states, the AI features "will come in two forms: first as a means of enhancing existing OS functionality with AI models in the background, and latterly in the form of 'AI native' features and workflows for those who want them."

These features will range from accessibility tools like improved speech-to-text and text-to-speech to agentic AI features for tasks …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/919411/canonical-ubuntu-linux-ai-features)
contentSnippet:
One of the most popular Linux distributions is about to get an influx of AI features. As reported by Phoronix, Jon Seager, VP of engineering at Ubuntu developer Canonical, shared a blog post on Monday detailing plans to add AI features to the Linux distro over the next year. As the post states, the AI features "will come in two forms: first as a means of enhancing existing OS functionality with AI models in the background, and latterly in the form of 'AI native' features and workflows for those who want them." 
These features will range from accessibility tools like improved speech-to-text and text-to-speech to agentic AI features for tasks …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Google employees ask Sundar Pichai to say no to classified military AI use
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![Sundar Pichai standing among seated attendees at the inauguration](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/04/gettyimages-2194442232.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Google CEO Sundar Pichai arrives for the inauguration of President Donald Trump. | Getty Images

Over 600 Google employees signed a letter to CEO Sundar Pichai demanding that Google block the Pentagon from using its AI models for classified purposes, reports the [_The Washington Post_](https://www.washingtonpost.com/technology/2026/04/27/google-employees-letter-ai-pentagon/). Its organizers claim many of the signers work in Google's DeepMind AI lab, and include more than 20 principals, directors, and vice presidents.

According to the _Post_, the letter says that "The only way to guarantee that Google does not become associated with such harms is to reject any classified workloads. Otherwise, such uses may occur without our knowledge or the power to stop them." Anthropic is currently in a legal battle with the Pentagon over being …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/919326/google-ai-pentagon-classified-letter)
